title: Toolchain Selection, Compatibility, and Reproducible Setup · Policy Workshop
summary: Select a minimal compatible toolchain and prove clean-machine setup. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a pinned bootstrap and compatibility matrix while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pin supported runtime and package-manager versions with one authoritative source.
Commit appropriate lockfiles and verify deterministic clean installs.
Choose tools with clear ownership and minimal duplicate diagnostics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.instruction:

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a gates-bootstrap-smoke-1inb0b: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Bootstrap Smoke into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-bootstrap-smoke-1inb0b:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.

Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Bootstrap Smoke condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Bootstrap Smoke applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test setup, build, and checks from a clean environment.
